scenario_results.reason: In-scope request produced an industrial construction quality or safety summary with permit, schedule, ITP, and turnover evidence linkage.
Missing permit and site-readiness prerequisites blocked progression and produced a dependency-based prerequisite gap output.
Open hold and failed inspection state were preserved; release was refused with exact blockers and named owners.
Contradictory field evidence downgraded confidence and prevented closure until the evidence chain is reconciled.
Recurring defect was escalated to formal corrective-action ownership instead of being silently closed.
Code and licensed-trade authority were refused and routed with a boundary-safe handoff package.
Safety or quality bypass request was rejected and the documented control boundary remained intact.
Missing tenant jurisdiction, release authority, and systems-of-record hierarchy triggered low-confidence escalation.
Completion and turnover were blocked until punch-list, redline, and acceptance evidence were complete and traceable.
Site and work-order handling used realistic industrial terminology and produced an evidence-backed control obligation summary.
Conflict between field practice and documented turnover policy was escalated instead of being resolved informally.
Permit, inspection, safety, or design authority changes caused a clean escalation to qualified owners rather than in-lane overreach.

notes: Scenario set covers normal execution, prerequisite gaps, hold enforcement, contradictory evidence, recurring defects, bypass refusal, closeout blockers, and authority escalation.
Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all quality-safety-and-assurance abilities, and all construction-and-field-services abilities required by the audit contract.
The package remains explicit that runtime is validated as a spec pack but still `specification-based` until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
